I think LeBron still drives to the basket a lot, but the difference (especially with Giannis) is that LeBron tries to get layups instead of dunks now. Giannis goes up with force to just jam it home, whereas LeBron tries to go for a layup right at the rim instead. LeBron really only tries to dunk it if the defense fell asleep and he has an empty lane to just jam it. Giannis much more looks to dunk around and over guys than LeBron does, and I think that extra aggression gets rewarded more than just having a soft touch off the glass for a lay in does.

Theres a bug difference between being a contender and building a dynasty. Winning a title, even with immeasurable talent, is hard. The Heat had arguably the most talent in the NBA and a Rockstar coach and could only win 2/4 titles. Kerr was instrumental in the Warriors Dynasty. There are other coaches who could have done that, but not many. Very good coaches with great talent struggle to even make the finals.

No it was purely about money. The salary cap rules at that time did not allow Cavs to go over the cap to resign him (because Boozer was a second round pick). So they let him out of the contract a year early, with the idea he would take the below market extension in exchange for being let out early. Instead he signed a huge contract (for the times) with the Jazz, and Cavs could not match because it would put them over the cap. This rule limiting resigning of second round picks was changed a few years later when Warriors lost Gilbert Arenas because they could not go over the cap to match his fre
